title: Cancer treatment for my family
story:
My name is Hana Yusuf and I am writing on behalf of my family who have been devastated by a series of catastrophic medical emergencies over the past eight months. In February 2026, my mother Fatima Yusuf, aged 67, was diagnosed with stage-three ovarian cancer at King's College Hospital in London. Her oncologist, Dr. Priya Nandakumar, prescribed an aggressive chemotherapy regimen followed by a targeted immunotherapy protocol — treatments that are only partially covered by NHS funding due to the experimental nature of the immunotherapy component.

The out-of-pocket costs have been staggering. The immunotherapy drug Atezolizumab, administered in six-week cycles, costs £8,400 per cycle. Dr. Nandakumar has prescribed a minimum of eight cycles, bringing that single line item to £67,200. On top of that, my mother requires specialist nursing care at home three days per week, billed at £320 per session, and private consultations averaging £450 each. Additional diagnostic imaging — PET-CT scans, MRI follow-ups — has added another £14,000 since February.

Meanwhile, in April 2026, my younger brother Khalid Yusuf, aged 29, was involved in a serious road traffic collision on the A23 near Croydon. He sustained multiple fractures requiring orthopaedic surgery at St George's Hospital, Tooting. His recovery has required physiotherapy three times per week for an estimated twelve months at £95 per session.

Our family's total medical debt across both cases now stands at approximately £185,000. We have exhausted all personal savings, remortgaged our home, and approached every charitable organisation we are aware of. Both cases qualify under the gharimin category as debt incurred through genuine necessity. Any contribution — however small — will go directly to the treatment fund administered by our family solicitor. Please help us keep our family together through this impossible crisis.
vouching_narrative: Our family is well-known within the Croydon Muslim community. The imam of Croydon Masjid, Sheikh Abdullah Farooqi, can confirm our circumstances, as can our neighbour and family friend Dr. Saira Malik who has visited my mother at home and witnessed her condition first-hand. The South London Islamic Relief Network has been informed of our case and their volunteer coordinator, Sister Nadia Hussain, can be contacted for further verification.
